title: tanacetum corymbosum subsp. achilleae
intro: Tanacetum corymbosum subsp. achilleae, also known as a subspecies of the White Tansy, is a plant species within the sunflower family that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Tanacetum corymbosum subsp. achilleae include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, among other benefits. The herbal preparation of this plant typically involves drying and infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea or tincture that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
